title: Topic, Headers, and Alternate Routing · Go Semantics Case: Overlapping Binding Analysis
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must parse topic segments, match bindings, model headers, and separate alternate custody from mandatory returns; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the overlapping binding analysis increment in a deterministic Go routing and unroutable-decision table. Learners p…
objectives: Enumerate overlapping topic bindings and prove which queues receive one copy even when multiple bindings on the same queue match; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Design bounded dot-separated topic taxonomies whose stable semantic segments support exact, star, and hash matching; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Overlapping Binding Analysis · Theory Overlapping Binding Analysis
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 9682 during table-driven topology review for a tenant migration. Build deterministic Go evidence to classify primary, alternate, returned, and invalid routing outcomes; retain rollback topology before deleting old bindings; then raise a broker resource alarm and halt new admission. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Enumerate overlapping topic bindings and prove which queues receive one copy even when multiple bindings on the same queue match; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
